body: Würde ich zu BankAccountInfos oder so umbenennen (ist ja kein DTO) und dann die bank-account.entity das Interface implementieren lassen. Dann haben wir da auch mehr Type-Safety.
diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+export interface BankAccountDto {

body:
Sorry, perhaps my comment wasn't clear: 
- @ApiProperty() should stay
- @IsNotEmptyObject(), @ValidateNested(), @Type(() => EntityDto) only for objects, not for enums

=> for the enum properties, all was correct 😉
How does this work with blanked mail and phone?
Let's say somebody enters the KYC Data on payment, but had mail and phone already saved. You will then call the /data endpoint and mail and phone are required, but you only have the blanked ones.
All 3 history DTOs are the same. Should we use a single class/interface?
I think it's a good thing to have very similar history entries for different routes. It might therefore be a good idea to have multiple DTOs, but use a common base class. WDYT?
Looks very similar to CreateBankAccountDto => use a common base class?
Add type here, and also add the ApiProperty to MinDeposit.

I would use the following fields:
- name
- street
- number
- zip
- city
- country
- iban
- bic
Perhaps `GetBuyPaymentInfoDto` would be a better name?
Perhaps `GetSellPaymentInfoDto` would be a better name?

Perhaps a description would help here?
```ts
@ApiProperty({ description: 'Source blockchain' })
```

Perhaps a description would help here?
```ts
@ApiProperty({ description: 'Target asset' })
```
I would move this to RouteHistoryDto. Maybe we can move the route-history.dto.ts to the history folder?
I would move this also to RouteHistoryDto and rename it to something like TypedHistoryDto?
It might be a good moment to also swagger-document the route history return DTOs 😉
Should we just use the chargebackBankTxId here (same as buyId in case of BuyCrypto)?
It's not a hash but a signature. So we can probably just reuse the normal AuthCredentialsDto?
Is it possible to change this to IsNotEmpty?
We should use only IDs here (for Google Sheets).
Add a TODO to switch to IsNotEmptyObject
Do we really need all these boolean validators? Can't it be done with a ValidateIf?
Why is this `string[]`, if it is comma separated? I would go for string array, as we are using a body here.

+

+  select?: string[]

body: `typeId` is not really a fitting name, sounds like id of the type 😁 maybe `originEntityId` or something?
I would use `@ApiProperty()` to make it clear that it should be required
In this case we can make it required. This functionality is only used by payment page.
Or make address methods (`isCardanoAddress`) on CryptoService `public static` and use them here.
Good idea! Maybe add a count to it?
`enumName: 'Blockchain'` should not be required 🤔 (for all enums)

I think this is not required here?

body: I would keep the default value `amount: number = 0;`, maybe some frontends don't specify the amount yet.
Is it `actors?: [{ id: string; label: string }];` or `actors?: { id: string; label: string }[];`
`Volume in CHF` and `Annual volume in CHF`
Do we need txId for input and output?
I would probably move this to the mapper file 🤔
remittanceInfo as inputTxId is weird 🤔 (same for buyFiat)
Filter for null can probably be removed everywhere in this file?
Convert to input asset as well?

`@ApiProperty({ description: 'Final rate (incl. fees) in source/target' })`
(same for sell + crypto)
Maybe we should rename it to something like `SignUpFee` or `CustomFee`?
If `manualRiskRate` is required we probably should rename the endpoint and DTO to setManualRisk? 😉
Decorators are useless since this is only used internally.

There are a lot of semicolons all over the place 😄  maybe we should keep it as a number array (or even FeeEntity array) in the DTO and only map it to string in the respective entities (BuyCrypto/BuyFiat)?
It's a bit weird to have `fixed` and `rate` (w/o fee), but also `minFee` (w/ fee) in the same DTO?
Leave the kycState there but set it to `NA`
Can we combine this with the KYC webhook data?

- Remove `INCORPORATION_CERTIFICATE`, will be named differently
- `FINANCIAL_DATA` can also be removed as it is not supported yet
Maybe use the name `targetAccount` (or something similar) and set it to the blockchain address for BuyCrypto?
I this DTO we use `input/output` instead of `source/target` 🤣  (description)
Couldn't you use `...(buyCrypto.outputAmount ? buyCrypto. buyCrypto.exchangeRate : null),` (same below)?

body: Maybe we should have a separate DTO for the reply, where the author can be specified? And maybe it should just be a string instead of enum?
This could then be removed again

- For BuyFiat, inputReferenceAsset is always the same as inputAsset
- For BuyCrypto, they might be different but the price source for conversion from input- to inputReferenceAsset is our bank

😅 
```ts
  private static StepMap: Record<EntityStatus, DtoStatus> = {
    [EntityStatus.NOT_STARTED]: DtoStatus.NOT_STARTED,
    [EntityStatus.IN_PROGRESS]: DtoStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
    [EntityStatus.FINISHED]: DtoStatus.IN_REVIEW,
    [EntityStatus.CHECK_PENDING]: DtoStatus.IN_REVIEW,

    [EntityStatus.OUTDATED]: DtoStatus.OUTDATED,

```

Master and slave doesn't really make sense from a integrator perspective. Maybe just call it `old` and `new` or similar?
Hm yes 🤔 I was thinking from the "slave" perspective. As a slave, the only change you see is a new account id 😄

body: Does it have to be a file, can't it just be JSON? 🤣
And maybe use a more meaningful name, e.g. `callData`?
I would call it BankAccount instead of BankData (for the user)
Might be a bit confusing to have both types in one DTO. Maybe create a separate DTO for the history and use a base class for the common fields?
Add a description (comma-separated list of statuses)
Fee cannot be edited in UI
`@IsEnum(PaymentStandard, { each: true })` (don't know if it works 😉)
Why nest it into an object with a single field?
I would use the same thing for completed as for not found (extend `ErrorDto`, in this case the error is `'Conflict'`)
There are some ZCHFs and XCHFs in this file 🤔
Just name it `uid` and set it below `id` (for all payment infos)

Total fee is an amount?
Use value from `mapFees`?
I think `entity.rate` is the total exchange rate, and rate here is the fee rate?
Maybe we should rename `BeneficialOwnerData` to something more generic?
It's all about users linked to an organization, right?

We would normally use
```ts
export enum TxStatementType {
  INVOICE = 'Invoice',
  RECEIPT = 'Receipt',
}
```
Why not remove this hierarchy and put the fields directly into the CreateCustodyOrderDto? It is the same for all transactions
SAVING_WITHDRAWAL is Withdraw from saving -> no IBAN required
I think it's only important for sell?
Descriptions are a mix between currency and asset
Validator decorators are not useful for internal-only DTOs (just use an interface)
I think we can omit the blockchain field in this case 🤔
Maybe it makes sense to not extend KycFileData and have a description on file/fileName fields (commercial register)?

body: I would do some rounding ... JS is very bad with divisions
What I meant is the thing a bit further down 😉 If done correctly, it e.g. looks like this
The controller documentation is very good indeed 😉 👍
Maybe we should just have a single field and detect what it is? Or will we have a type-selection in the frontend?
I would not use a default here, that's a bit shady 😉
Is this meant to be my own alias?

The historical prices are still date + time, right (at least in the DB)? So we probably wouldn't find something in the map here?
In CustodyService we solved it with `Util.isoDate` as map key.

body: Use language from user data instead
This DTO is a requirement of Aktionariat
I think we can remove the Swagger docu here, this price is not really important
I would not use fallbacks (just leave undefined)
How can this throw an error?
Is a bit dangerous when the user can override the chargeback amount?
Makes sense, but the "normal" (non-employee) user should not be able to override the chargeback amount for his transactions.
